steps.title: Use a custom regression row
steps.content: For infinitely many solutions, both sides must match for every $x$. In Desmos, use two quick test inputs: enter $x_1=[0,1]$, then type $18(x_1+5)\sim2(x_1+c)+16x_1$. Desmos solves the unknown constant and returns $c=45$.

steps.title: Confirm with coefficients
steps.content: Expanding gives $$18(x+5)=18x+90$$ and $$2(x+c)+16x=18x+2c.$$ The $x$-coefficients are already both $18$, so only the constants need to match: $$90=2c$$ $$c=45$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The value $c=45$ matches choice B. Substituting $c=45$ gives $18x+90=18x+90$, an identity, so the equation has infinitely many solutions.
